sections.paragraph:
ἑκάτερος, α, ον (Pind. et al.; pap, LXX; En 24:2; Test12Patr; ApcrEzk [Epiph. 70, 16]; EpArist; Philo, Sacr. Abel. 138; Jos., Ant. 12, 239; Tat.; Mel. P. 48, 341 [Christ.]; Ath.) each of two, both Dg 12:4; Agr 2.—DELG s.v. ἕκαστος.

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

ApcrEzk ApcrEzk = Apocryphon Ezechiel—List 2

Epiph Epiph , IV–V a.d.—List 5

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

Christ. Christ. = Christian

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

Agr Agr = Agraphon (non-canonical sayings of Jesus)—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 298
